reasoning: Primary credit borrowing at the discount window strongly exhibits seasonal year-end spikes followed by a steady draining of liquidity throughout Q1. The FRED data shows a massive spike to nearly $10,000M at the end of December 2025, which has aggressively mean-reverted downward (dropping to $3,498M by Jan 21, before a minor month-end bump to $4,516M on Jan 28). Historically (e.g., Q1 2025), balances naturally settle into the $2,000M - $3,000M range by February and March. Because the baseline on 2026-02-01 will likely capture the elevated late-January month-end reading (or even if it captures early February, which is on a downward trajectory), the series is highly favored to decrease by Feb 8 and remain lower by March 3. I am estimating a 30% chance of an increase by Feb 8 (the brief interval makes a continuation of the post-month-end burnoff extremely likely) and a 40% chance of an increase by March 3 (approaching closer to 0.5 due to higher general uncertainty and potential early-March month-end noise, though still favoring the structural Q1 downtrend).
